title: Is There a Causal Relation between Maternal Acetaminophen Administration and ADHD?
ehr_evidence: None. The study uses a murine model (CD1 mice).
summary: An experimental study using a murine model to investigate whether prenatal exposure to acetaminophen (APAP) causes hyperkinetic dysfunction (ADHD-like behavior) in offspring.
key_findings: No significant differences in locomotor activity between APAP-exposed and control mouse offspring.
No differences in volumes of 29 brain areas of interest on MRI.
No differences in neuronal quantifications between groups.
limitations: Lack of accurate assessment of ADHD in murine models
Results should be taken with caution when compared to clinical data
data_availability_statement: All relevant data are within the paper and its Supporting Information files.
